commands.review_summary:
The PR moves plugin registry, configured plugin install, and legacy plugin manifest Doctor repairs onto the internal structured health-check runner and adds focused coverage for findings, repairs, effects, diffs, and validation.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a structured Doctor repair conversion PR, not a bug report with a current-main failing reproduction. The useful verification path is source/contract review plus current-head real Doctor CLI proof.
commands.review_followup: Protected maintainer-labeled stacked PR needs human contract/landing-order review and current-head proof, not an automated repair lane.

target.title: Doctor: continue structured repair conversion
target.body:
## Summary

This is the next Doctor repair-stack PR after #85566.

It moves plugin registry, configured plugin install, and legacy plugin manifest repairs onto the internal normalized Doctor runner path while keeping the public plugin health contract unchanged:

- legacy plugin manifests remain split detect/repair because file discovery and file rewrite are naturally separable
- configured plugin install repair runs as an internal core runner check at its existing positional contribution point
- plugin registry refresh runs as an internal core runner check at its existing positional contribution point

The public plugin-facing health contract remains split `detect(ctx, scope?)` plus optional `repair(ctx, findings)`. This PR does not add public SDK `run(ctx)` support and does not update generated plugin SDK baselines.

## Review Notes

This PR keeps the legacy Doctor order isomorphic by routing these repairs through their existing contribution positions instead of adding one broad early repair pass. Lint remains findings-oriented; fix owns mutation; dry-run/diff are derived from returned changes, effects, and diffs.

## Verification

- `git diff --check fork/doctor-core-run-contract-migration...HEAD`
- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental false --pretty false`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental false --pretty false`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/commands/doctor-plugin-manifests.test.ts src/commands/doctor-plugin-manifests.ts src/commands/doctor-plugin-registry.test.ts src/commands/doctor-plugin-registry.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts src/flows/health-check-runner-types.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run src/commands/doctor-plugin-manifests.test.ts src/commands/doctor-plugin-registry.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.test.ts --reporter=dot --pool=threads --fileParallelism=false`

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior addressed: Plugin registry, configured plugin install, and legacy plugin manifest Doctor repairs now return structured runner data for findings, changes, effects, diffs, warnings, and validation while preserving their existing repair positions.

Real environment tested: WSL Ubuntu 24.04 source checkout at `/root/src/openclaw-84290-doctor-next`, stacked on #85566 head `d64ff361f73129b7dd50809fdaee307a9c9b3d36`, current PR head `42238690404d10b3d5a79332c80fac7577f8a480`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Replayed only the two plugin-registry PR commits onto current PR2, resolved the old public-contract conflicts in favor of internal runner types, ran core/test typecheck, checked formatting, and ran focused Vitest coverage for plugin manifest repair, plugin registry repair, core health checks, doctor health contributions, repair flow, and lint flow.

Evidence after fix: Focused Vitest passed for 6 files / 79 tests; core test-project `tsgo` passed; core production `tsgo` passed; oxfmt passed on all 10 touched files; git diff checks passed.

Observed result after fix: The plugin repair checks are structured through the internal runner, positional Doctor contribution order is preserved, and no public plugin SDK health surface or generated SDK baseline changes are included.

What was not tested: No full `pnpm check`, no full Vitest suite, no remote Crabbox/Testbox run for this PR update yet.
target.author: giodl73-repo

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
